Performances by professional actors are central to the experience, not forgetting a fun-filled quiz with prizes for the winners. Can be a bit formal at times but this one's been going a long time and is well worth the experience for such an unusual tour. There's just enough time to stop in each pub for a pint as well. }}
=== Performing Arts === * {{do | name=Abbey Theatre | alt=National Theatre of Ireland | url=https://abbeytheatre.ie/ | email= | address=26/27 Lower Abbey St D01 K0F1 | lat=53.3483 | long=-6.2572 | directions= | phone=+353 1 878 7222 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Abbey Theatre | image=wikidata=Q306434 | lastedit=2024-06-06 | content=Opened in 1904, and always at the centre of the Irish drama scene, the Abbey is a good venue for catching local playwrights' work but with a global repertoire. It's intended to replace the current building with two new theatres fronting onto the Liffey. This will mean a lengthy closure, but the work has not started. }} * {{do | name=Gaiety Theatre | alt= | url=https://www.gaietytheatre.ie | email= | address=46 King St South D02 V097 | lat=53.3403 | long=-6.2616 | directions= | phone=+353 1 646 8600 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Gaiety Theatre, Dublin | wikidata=Q585698 | lastedit=2024-06-06 | content=Founded in 1871 so this is the oldest continually operating theatre in Dublin. It hosts popular musical shows, opera, ballet, dance and drama. }} * {{do | name=Gate Theatre | alt= | url=https://www.gatetheatre.ie | email= | address=8 Parnell Square East D01 T8K1 | lat=53.3531 | long=-6.2619 | directions= | phone=+353 1 874 4045 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Gate Theatre | wikidata=Q728893 | lastedit=2024-06-06 | content=This has a focus on European and American theatre ranging from classics to modern plays. It was established as a theatre company in 1928 by Hilton Edward and Micheál MacLiammóir. }} * {{do | name=National Concert Hall | alt= | url=https://www.nch.ie/ | email= | address=Earlsfort Terrace D02 N527 | lat=53.3347 | long=-6.2589 | directions= | phone=+353 1 417 0077 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=National Concert Hall | lastedit=2024-06-06 | content=This offers classical concerts. Frequent performances by the resident orchestra, the RTÉ National Symphony Orchestra. The main auditorium seats 1200 and there are three smaller spaces. It was built in 1865 for a trade fair then became an entertainment venue then the core of University College Dublin. It became a concert hall from 1981 when UCD moved to the Belfield campus. }} [[File:Bord Gais Theatre, Dublin, Ireland.jpg | thumb | 300px | Bord Gáis Energy Theatre ]] * {{do | name=Bord Gáis Energy Theatre | alt=Grand Canal Theatre | url=https://bordgaisenergytheatre.ie/ | email= | address=Grand Canal Square D02 PA03 | lat=53.3443 | long=-6.2401 | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 7999 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Bord Gáis Energy Theatre | wikidata=Q4944429 | lastedit=2024-06-06 | content=The theatre offers a wide range of shows featuring ballet, musicals, family shows, drama, concerts, comedy and opera. With a 2100-seater auditorium, the building was designed by Daniel Libeskind and completed in 2010. }} * {{do | name=The Helix | alt= | url=https://thehelix.ie/ | email=info@thehelix.dcu.ie | address=DCU Glasnevin Campus, Collins Avenue D09 FW22 | lat=53.3866 | long=-6.2590 | directions= | phone=+353 1 700 7000 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=The_Helix, Dublin | wikidata=Q7739337 | lastedit=2024-12-09 | content=Event venue and theatre located in the northern suburbs of the city, built in 1996. Hosts a wide variety of talks and performances. }} * '''[http://www.gaytheatre.ie/ International Dublin Gay Theatre Festival]''' is held in May, celebrating the contribution of gay people to theatre, past and present. The event was founded in 2004 to mark the 150th anniversary of the birth of Oscar Wilde in his native city. It has an emphasis on new International and Irish works with a broadly gay theme or relevance.
=== Sports === {{infobox|Bloody Sunday|Sun 21 Nov 1920 saw shocking killings in Dublin. Ireland was still part of the UK but had declared independence, and civil war broke out. That Sunday morning, the IRA killed 16 British intelligence officers in Dublin. In the afternoon, Dublin had a Gaelic football match against Tipperary at Croke Park. Police, army and "Black and Tan" paramilitaries arrived mob-handed, officially to search the spectators for suspects. They began firing indiscriminately into the crowd: 12 were fatally shot (including a Tipperary player) and two more were crushed in the stampede to flee. It was a huge blow to the legitimacy of British rule. See [[Thurles]] for more on the link between Gaelic football and Irish identity. }} * '''Gaelic Games:''' {{do | name=Croke Park Stadium | alt= | url=https://crokepark.ie/ | email= | address=Jones Rd D03 P6K7 | lat=53.3608 | long=-6.2511 | directions= | phone=+353 1 819 2300 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Croke Park | wikidata=Q478225 | lastedit=2024-06-06 | content=This stages Gaelic football and hurling for Dublin County GAA and national competitions, with a playing season April-Sep. The stadium has a capacity of 82,300 yet you may struggle to get tickets for big games. Tours of the stadium and [https://crokepark.ie/gaamuseum GAA museum] are also available, including a chance to try out these sports. You can also take the "skyline walk" across the stadium roof. }} * '''Rugby union:''' {{do | name=Leinster Rugby | alt= | url=https://www.leinsterrugby.ie/ | email= | address=RDS Arena, Anglesea Rd, Ballsbridge D04 AK83 | lat=53.3256 | long=-6.2294 | directions=5 km south of centre, Bus 4, 7, 18 | phone=+353 1 668 0866 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Leinster play in the United Rugby Championship, the professional European league, and often win it. The stadium has a capacity of 18,500 and big games sometimes transfer to Aviva stadium. }} * {{marker | name=[https://www.avivastadium.ie/ Aviva Stadium] | type=do | lat=53.3353 | long=-6.2284 }} on Lansdowne Rd D04 W2F3 is Ireland's national stadium, capacity 51,700. International rugby, soccer and other big events are hosted here. It's a mile southeast of the centre, take the DART train to Lansdowne Rd or buses towards Sandymount or Ballsbridge. * '''Football:''' the soccer season in the Republic is March-November, with games usually on Friday evenings at 7:45PM. : {{do | name=Shamrock Rovers | alt= | url=https://www.shamrockrovers.ie/ | email= | address=Tallaght Stadium, Whitestown Way D24 FNK6 | lat=53.2835 | long=-6.3735 | directions=Tram to The Square | phone=+353 1 420 3033 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=They play in the League of Ireland Premier Division, the top tier. The stadium (capacity 10,500) is on N81 two km west of M50. }} : '''[https://shelbournefc.ie/ Shelbourne FC]''' also play in the Premier Division. Their stadium (capacity 5700) is Tokla Park in Drumcondra, 5 km north of city centre. * {{do | name=Leopardstown Racecourse | alt= | url=http://www.leopardstown.com | email=info@leopardstown.com | address=Leopardstown D18 V9C6 | lat=53.2658 | long=-6.1892 | directions=Follow N11 south, turn right into R113 Leopardstown Road | phone=+353 1 289 0500 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | wikipedia=Leopardstown Racecourse | wikidata=Q6526888 | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=This has regular meetings throughout the year, flat-racing in summer and jumps in winter. There's a "Pay as you Play" golf course within the racecourse grounds, as well as bars, restaurants and a nightclub. }} * '''Golf:''' some two dozen golf courses ring the city. Best known is the [https://www.theroyaldublingolfclub.com/ Royal Dublin] on the links of Bull Island. * '''Cricket:''' Ireland plays international matches at [[Malahide]], northeast edge of the city.
=== Other === * '''Cinema:''' the most central mainstream cinemas are north side: Savoy on O'Connell St, Cineworld on Parnell St, and Lighthouse by the Jameson Distillery. * {{do | name=Irish Film Institute | alt= | url=https://www.ifi.ie/ | email= | address=6 Eustace Street, Temple Bar D02 PD85 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 679 5744 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Arthouse cinema for independent Irish and international movies. }} * {{do | name=Conradh na Gaeilge | alt=Gaelic League | url=https://cnag.ie | email= | address=6 Harcourt St D02 VH98 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 475 7401 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Cultural centre where Irish is the first language. They organise events and classes. }}
== Buy ==
Dublin is not cheap, but visitors from outside the European Union can obtain a refund of VAT on some goods, which in 2024 is 23%. Ask the shop for details: the refund is usually applied at point of sale, or you may be issued a voucher to claim it on leaving the country. Visitors from Great Britain are eligible, but not those from Northern Ireland. More on VAT refunds can be found on the Irish [https://www.citizensinformation.ie/en/travel-and-recreation/travel-to-ireland/tax-free-purchases-for-non-eu-travellers/ Government website].
=== South side === [[File:Grafton St, Dublin.jpg | thumb | 300px | Grafton Street ]] * '''Grafton Street''' is the principal shopping street. It courses south from Trinity College (junction with Temple Bar) for a block to the corner of Nassau St, and beyond it's pedestrianised to St Stephen's Green. : {{buy | name=Brown Thomas | alt= | url=https://www.brownthomas.com/ | email= | address=88-95 Grafton Street D02 VF65 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 605 6666 | tollfree= | hours=M Tu 10AM-7PM, W-F 9AM-8PM, Sa 10AM-8PM, Su 11AM-7PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=Dublin's best-known and expensive department store. }} * '''Nassau Street''' branches east from Grafton St to form the south boundary of Trinity College. It retails giftware such as Waterford Crystal, Belleek Pottery, Aran sweaters, and other craft items. Easons at #24 is a well-stocked bookstore. : {{buy | name=Kilkenny Design | alt= | url=http://www.kilkennygroup.com/ | email= | address=6 Nassau Street D02 W865 | lat=53.3422 | long=-6.2552 | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 7066 | tollfree= | hours=Daily 9:30AM-6PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=Clothing, jewellery and giftware. }} * '''Dawson Street''' branches off Nassau St to run south parallel to Grafton St. It has clothes, music and book shops, restaurants and Mansion House the Lord Mayor's residence. : {{buy | name=Hodges Figgis | alt= | url=https://www.hodgesfiggis.ie/ | email= | address=56 Dawson Street D02 XE81 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 4754 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 9AM-7PM, Sa 9AM-6PM, Su 10:30AM-6PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=Well-stocked large bookshop, part of the Waterstones chain. }} * {{buy | name=Powerscourt Centre | alt= | url=https://www.powerscourtcentre.ie/ | email= | address=59 South William Street D02 HF95 | lat=53.3422 | long=-6.2619 | directions= | phone=+353 1 679 4144 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 10AM-6PM, Su noon-6PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=Attractive shopping centre in a beautifully restored 18th-century townhouse, with clothes, cafes, galleries and jewellers. You're paying premium prices for the elegant location. }} * {{buy | name=George's Street Arcade | alt=Market Arcade | url=https://www.georgesstreetarcade.ie/ | email= | address=South Great George's St | lat= | long= | directions=block west of Powerscourt Centre | phone= | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 9AM-PM, Su 11AM-PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=A covered red-brick shopping arcade, with vintage clothing, fabrics, unusual accessories, vinyl and club wear. It also has some small cafes. }} * '''[https://freshthegoodfoodmarket.ie/ Fresh]''' is a gourmet grocery chain with nine city outlets. The most central south-side are on Grand Canal Square and on Lower Camden St. * '''Temple Bar''' has small independent shops along Temple Lane, Crow Street and Fownes Street. : '''Markets''' are held on Saturday on Temple Bar Square and Meetinghouse Square, mostly food items. : '''Cow's Lane Design Market''' is held on the street on Saturday. It's west edge of Temple Bar approaching Christ Church.
=== North side === [[File:Moore Street market, Dublin.jpg | thumb | 300px | Fruit & veg market on Moore St ]] Shopping north of the river is focussed in the quarter bounded by O'Connell Street to the east (for green line trams), Parnell St to the north, Jervis St to the west and Abbey St to the south (for red line trams). Henry Street runs east-west through the quarter, and off it is Moore Street, with a fruit, vegetable and fish market.
* {{buy | name=Arnott's | alt= | url=https://www.arnotts.ie/ | email= | address=12 Henry St D01 C3Y9 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 805 0400 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 10AM-7PM, Su 11AM-7PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-20 | content=A large department store with a long history. }} * {{buy | name=Jervis Shopping Centre | alt= | url=https://jervis.ie/ | email= | address=Jervis St D01 W3X5 | lat= | long= | directions=or enter from Abbey St | phone=+353 1 878 1323 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 9AM-7PM, Su 11AM-:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=Shopping centre with some 50 stores. }} * {{buy | name=Ilac Centre | alt= | url=https://www.ilac.ie/ | email= | address=Henry St D01 HW54 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 828 8900 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 8:30AM-8PM, Su 11AM-9PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=This shopping centre has some 40 stores. The city Central Library is on the first floor. }} * {{buy | name=Chapters Bookstore | alt= | url=https://chaptersbookstore.com/ | email= | address=Ivy Exchange, Parnell Street D01 P8C2 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 879 2700 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 9AM-6PM, Su noon-6PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-12 | content=Huge selection of books at keen prices, as well as a large secondhand section. }} * '''Talbot Street''' runs east from this quarter to Connolly railway station. Lots of little nicknack stores.
=== Elsewhere === * '''The Liberties''' are in the west city, with small stores and market stalls around Thomas Street and Meath Street. * '''Further out''' are edge-of-city malls. The largest are at Blanchardstown (off N3), Liffey Valley (junction of M50 and N4), The Square Tallaght (off N81), and Dundrum Town Centre (south on Luas green line).
== Eat == {{mapframe|53.345|-6.265|height=500|width=500|name=You won't go hungry in Dublin|zoom=14|show=eat}} Dublin has a wide range of eating places in all price brackets, though visitors may consider them overpriced by European standards – one factor is VAT of 23%.
Cuisines such as Indian and Chinese were slow to appear as Dublin only had small ethnic communities, but they now have a good presence especially north of the river. Indian food is generally North Indian. Chinese food in Ireland has become something of its own thing, with the omnipresent "Spice Bag" an idiosyncratic new food of 21st century Ireland. Vegetarian, vegan and GF options are also now readily available.

== Drink == {{mapframe|53.345|-6.265|height=500|width=500|name=A select few of Dublin's pubs|zoom=14|show=drink}} '''Water''' in Dublin is fresh and clean from the Wicklow Mountains. The water of Vartry reservoir at [[Roundwood]] is commended in Joyce's ''Ulysses'' but the characters go boozing instead. Poulaphouca at [[Blessington]] was built later, and Sally Gap is the scenic road between the two catchments.
'''Pubs:''' it's reckoned that Dublin has over 600. You can but make a start.
In 2021 you might pay €6 for a pint, glass of wine or measure of spirits, somewhat more in Temple Bar. Pubs are generally open Su-Th to 23:00 and F Sa to 01:00 or later. Hours were curtailed during covid, the plus (which will likely remain) was that pubs increased their food offering, which meant they were "restaurants" and permitted to stay open - "You must try our pizza!" became a legally enforceable invitation. Smoking is illegal within all Irish pubs but many have a beer garden or similar outdoor smoking area.
'''Temple Bar''' was named for the sand bar and mud flats along the south bank of the Liffey, reclaimed for building in the 17th century. "Temple" was both the name of the landowners, and of the Temple Bar district in London, with Essex St and Fleet St in the same respective positions. It's nowadays a tourist strip of cobbled alleys, drinking places, restaurants, more drinking places, shops, even more drinking places . . . it's very central so those stag and hen parties are probably just in for a quick one before heading to the Book of Kells or the big museums. Take care, but the sheer number of people on the streets gives you a measure of safety.
=== Traditional Irish === * {{drink | name=Peadar Kearney's | alt= | url=http://www.peadarkearneys.ie/ | email= | address=64 Dame St D02 RT72 | lat=53.344273 | long=-6.265415 | directions=Temple Bar | phone=+353 1 670 6457 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 11AM-11:30PM, F Sa 11AM-12:30AM, Su noon-11:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Named for Peadar Ó Cearnaígh (1883-1942) who penned ''Amhráin na bhFiann'', Ireland's national anthem, and was uncle to the three Behans, authors and prolific drinkers all. The pub is a great spot for pre- and post-gig drinks next to Olympia Theatre, with a young crowd and live music from up and coming Irish trad bands. Mostly tourists here but a nice spot to talk to other visitors. }} * {{drink | name=The Cobblestone | alt= | url=http://cobblestonepub.ie/ | email= | address=77 North King St D07 TP22 | lat=53.3498 | long=-6.2778 | directions=Smithfield Square | phone=+353 1 872 1799 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 2-11:30PM, Su 1-11:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=They describe themselves as "a drinking pub with a music problem" and have nightly trad sessions. }} * {{drink | name=Frank Ryans | alt= | url=https://www.frankryanspizza.ie/ | email= | address=5 Queen St D07 D227 | lat=53.3469 | long=-6.2801 | directions= | phone=+353 89 217 3073 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 4-11:30PM, Su 6-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=This pub and pizzeria has friendly bar staff and a mixed crowd of students, law types, trendies and locals. Expect weekly trad nights interspersed with Rockabilly, Country and Soul on the jukebox. }} * {{drink | name=O'Donoghue's | alt= | url=https://www.odonoghues.ie/ | email= | address=15 Merrion Row D02 PF50 | lat=53.3382 | long=-6.2542 | directions= | phone=+353 1 660 7194 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-midnight, F Sa 10:30-1AM, Su 12:30AM-midnight | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=With two bars across a courtyard, it's famous for impromptu live music, and where folk group The Dubliners were formed. They have rooms. }} * {{drink | name=The Barge | alt=McCafferty's | url=https://www.mccaffertysatthebarge.com/ | email= | address=42 Charlemont St D02 R593 | lat=53.330551 | long=-6.260588 | directions=by canal and Clayton Hotel | phone=+353 1 475 1869 | tollfree= | hours=Su-W noon-11:30PM, Th-Sa noon-2:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Friendly trad pub with four areas, good food and great decor. }} [[File:TempleBar TheOliver.JPG | thumb | 300px | Colorful pubs in Temple Bar ]] * {{drink | name=Hartigan's | alt= | url= | email= | address=100 Leeson St Lower D02 W023 | lat=53.3360 | long=-6.2567 | directions= | phone=+353 1 443 9541 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 12:30-11:30PM, F Sa 12:30-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Popular student bar, good choice after international rugby matches. }} * {{drink | name=The Brazen Head | alt= | url=https://www.brazenhead.com/ | email= | address=20 Bridge Street Lower D08 WC64 | lat=53.3450 | long=-6.2764 | directions= | phone=+353 1 679 5186 | tollfree= | hours=Daily noon-11:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Surely the oldest pub in Dublin, founded in 1198 - Ireland's oldest, in [[Athlone]], is from 900 AD. Wonderful on warm, dry summer nights. Live trad music and friendly atmosphere. One of the bars is covered in signed currency notes, there's quite a collection but lacking any bitcoins. }} * {{drink | name=Ned O'Shea's | alt= | url=https://www.nedosheas.ie/ | email= | address=12 Bridge Street Lower D08 Y271 | lat=53.344891 | long=-6.275900 | directions=opposite Brazen Head | phone=+353 86 040 5763 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th noon-11:30PM, F-Su 8AM-midnight | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Decent food and live music and dancing. Ned O’Shea (1937-2018) was a famous trad musician from Kerry. }} * {{drink | name=John Fallon's | alt=Capstan Bar | url=https://johnfallon.ie/ | email= | address=129 The Coombe D08 Y8CP | lat=53.338984 | long=-6.273376 | directions=opposite St Patrick's Cathedral | phone=+353 1 454 2801 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th 12:30-11:30PM, F Sa 12:30-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Small friendly local pub. }} * {{drink | name=The Oval | alt= | url=http://theovalbar.com/ | email= | address=78 Middle Abbey St D01 RW24 | lat=53.348436 | long=-6.260338 | directions=corner with O'Connell St | phone=+353 1 872 1264 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-11:30PM, F Sa 10:30AM-12:30AM, Su 12:30-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Good for drink and food, does an excellent Irish stew. Attracts a mixed age group. Lots of pictures of old Irish celebrities with a tribute to the Quiet Man. }} * {{drink | name=John Kavanagh's | alt=The Gravediggers | url=https://www.facebook.com/JohnKavanaghTheGravediggers | email= | address=1 Prospect Square, Glasnevin D09 CF72 | lat=53.3696 | long=-6.2719 | directions=by Glasnevin cemetery | phone= | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 10:30AM-10:30PM, Su 12:30-10:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Mournfully authentic pub from 1833 and little altered, where you toast the departed in the next-door cemetery. }} * {{drink | name=Bowe's Lounge Bar | alt= | url=http://www.bowespub.com/ | email= | address=31 Fleet St D02 DF77 | lat=53.345826 | long=-6.257883 | directions=by Trinity College | phone=+353 1 671 4038 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 12:30-11:30PM, F Sa 12:30-12:30AM, Su 3-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Victorian pub with a huge selection of whiskey. }} * {{drink | name=Mulligans | alt= | url=https://www.facebook.com/people/Mulligans/100064593853632/ | email= | address=Poolbeg St D02 TK71 | lat=53.346922 | long=-6.255528 | directions=by Tara St station | phone=+353 1 677 5582 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th noon-11:30PM, F Sa 11AM-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Busy pub with great Guinness and plenty of history, having been frequented by James Joyce among others. }} * {{drink | name=Nancy Hands | alt= | url=https://www.nancyhands.ie | email= | address=30 Parkgate St D08 W6X3 | lat=53.3482 | long=-6.2939 | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 0149 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th noon-11:30PM, F Sa noon-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Family-friendly restaurant and bar by entrance to Phoenix Park. }} * {{drink | name=Ryan's | alt= | url=https://www.thebuckleycollection.ie/parkgate-street | email= | address=28 Parkgate St D08 CH93 | lat=53.348199 | long=-6.29346 | directions=next to Nancy Hands | phone=+353 1 677 6097 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa noon-10PM, Su 12:30-9PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Beautiful Victorian pub, part of Buckley Steakhouse chain. A good place for a pint or meal before getting a train out of Heuston. }} * {{drink | name=Palace Bar | alt= | url=https://www.thepalacebardublin.com/ | email= | address=21 Fleet St D02 H950 | lat=53.345887 | long=-6.259611 | directions=Temple Bar | phone=+353 1 671 7388 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th noon-11:30PM, F Sa noon-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Trad bar with interesting decor complete with "snug" (small private booth). Live music upstairs. }} * {{drink | name=The Long Hall | alt= | url= | email= | address=31 South Great Georges St D02 DV744 | lat=53.3418 | long=-6.2653 | directions= | phone=+353 1 475 1590 | tollfree= | hours=Daily noon-11:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Atmospheric bar with Victorian decor, one of the last "long hall" bars in Ireland. }} * {{drink | name=Kehoe's | alt= | url=https://kehoesdublin.ie/ | email= | address=9 Anne St South D02 NY88 | lat=53.3412 | long=-6.2595 | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 8312 | tollfree= | hours=Daily M-Th 10AM-11:30PM, F Sa 10AM-12:30PM, Su 11:30AM-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=An excellent spot for a pint or a meal after a days' sight-seeing or shopping. Several snugs downstairs. }} [[File:Glasnevin Cemetery, officially known as Prospect Cemetery (4165390520).jpg | thumb | 300px | Toast the departed at Kavanagh's, Glasnevin ]] * {{drink | name=Kennedy's | alt= | url=https://kennedyspub.ie/ | email= | address=30 Westland Row D02 DP70 | lat=53.3422 | long=-6.2508 | directions=rear of Trinity College | phone=+353 1 679 9077 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-11:30PM, F Sa 10:30-12:30AM, Su noon-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Trad-style pub serving good food and drink with plenty of friendly atmosphere. }} * {{drink | name=O'Neill's | alt= | url=http://www.oneillspubdublin.com/ | email= | address=Suffolk St D02 KX03 | lat=53.343834 | long=-6.260743 | directions=by Molly Malone | phone=+353 1 679 3656 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-11:30PM, F Sa 10:30-AM-12:30AM, Su 11:30AM-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Excellent atmosphere in a Victorian style pub. They have great food with weekend lunchtime carvery. }} * {{drink | name=The Stag's Head | alt= | url=https://stagshead.ie/ | email= | address=1 Dame Court D02 TW84 | lat=53.3438 | long=-6.2636 | directions=off Great Georges St | phone=+353 1 679 3687 | tollfree= | hours=M-F noon-midnight, Sa 11AM-1AM< Su 11AM-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Trad Victorian pub, no TV, just great ales and conversation. }} * {{drink | name=Dawson Lounge | alt= | url=https://www.facebook.com/TheDawsonLounge | email= | address=25 Dawson St D02 XT59 | lat=53.3398 | long=-6.2589 | directions= | phone=+353 1 671 0311 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 4-11:30PM, F Sa noon-12:30AM, Su 12:30-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Probably Dublin's smallest pub - 20 customers and it's packed. }} * {{drink | name=McDaids | alt= | url= | email= | address=3 Harry Street D02 NC42 | lat=53.341237 | long=-6.260997 | directions=opposite Bruxelles | phone=+353 1 670 4395 | tollfree= | hours=M-W 2-11PM, Th noon-midnight, Su noon-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=This trad pub was a regular place for Brendan Behan and Oscar Wilde to ponder life. }} * {{drink | name=Grogan's | alt=Castle Lounge | url=https://www.groganspub.ie/ | email= | address=15 William St South D02 H336 | lat=53.3422 | long=-6.2627 | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 9320 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-11:30PM, F Sa 10:30AM-12:30AM, Su 12:30-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Wonderful trad pub, no music or TV. Great Guinness and a mixture of tourists and locals, with a hotchpotch of interesting art on the walls. }}
=== Modern === * {{drink | name=The Bailey | alt= | url=https://www.baileybarcafe.com/ | email= | address=1-4 Duke St D02 ET99 | lat=53.342014 | long=-6.259391 | directions=off Grafton St | phone=+353 1 670 4939 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th noon-11PM, F-Su noon-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-14 | content=Contemporary bar with tapas, presided over by a portrait of James Joyce. Nice outdoor seating, very busy on summer afternoons and evenings. }} * {{drink | name=The Lotts | alt= | url=https://thelottscafebar.com/ | email= | address=9 Liffey St Lower D01 E3F9 | lat=53.347383 | long=-6.263319 | directions=100 m north of Ha'penny Bridge | phone=+353 1 872 7669 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th 11AM-8PM, F Sa 11AM-midnight | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Cafe bar, elegant interior with chandeliers, a marble bar and comfortable leather seating. Live music many nights. Small outside seating area. }} * {{drink | name=Market Bar | alt= | url=https://www.marketbar.ie | email= | address=14a Fade St D02 A368 | lat=53.3421 | long=-6.2641 | directions=in George's St Arcade | phone=+353 1 613 9094 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th 12:30-11:30PM, F Sa 12:30PM-1:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Spacious bar and tapas restaurant with a good value menu. }} * {{drink | name=The Odeon | alt= | url=https://odeon.ie | email= | address=57 Harcourt St D02 VE22 | lat=53.3333 | long=-6.2624 | directions= | phone=+353 1 478 2091 | tollfree= | hours=Tu W noon-11:30PM, Th F noon-2:30AM, Sa 3PM-2:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=This attractive bar at the top of Harcourt St is in a converted railway station. }} * {{drink | name=Pygmalion | alt= | url=https://pyg.ie | email= | address=59 South William St D02 HK51 | lat=53.3422 | long=-6.2623 | directions=in Powerscourt Townhouse shopping centre | phone=+353 1 633 4522 | tollfree= | hours=M-W 5PM-3AM, Th F 3PM-3AM, Sa 1PM-3AM, Su 1PM-1:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Busy bar and restaurant with Med food, best book if you hope to eat. }} * {{drink | name=Café en Seine | alt= | url=https://www.cafeenseine.ie/ | email= | address=39 Dawson St D02 X067 | lat=53.340723 | long=-6.258600 | directions=100 m north of St Stephens Green | phone=+353 1 677 4567 | tollfree= | hours=Su-Th 11:30AM-11:30PM, F Sa 11:30AM-2:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Swish megapub on 3 floors, pricey. }}
=== Micro-breweries === [[File:Dublin - Guinness Storehouse - 20160507154437.jpg | thumb | 300px | St James Gate Storehouse ]] * {{drink | name=Against the Grain | alt= | url=http://galwaybaybrewery.com/againstthegrain/ | email= | address=11 Wexford St D02 HY84 | lat=53.3371 | long=-6.2654 | directions= | phone=+353 1 470 5100 | tollfree= | hours=W-F 4PM-midnight, Sa noon-1AM, Su 1-11:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Owned by a Galway brewery, this has a wide variety of Irish micro-brews and world beers. No generic commercial beers on tap. A vibrant pub with an eclectic clientele. }} * {{drink | name=Bull and Castle | alt= | url=https://www.thebuckleycollection.ie/bull-castle-steakhouse | email= | address=5 Lord Edward St D02 P634 | lat= | long= | directions=next to Christ Church | phone=+353 1 475 1122 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa noon-10PM, Su 12:30-9PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Gastropub and beer hall with a large selection of micro-brewed and international beers. Part of Buckley Steakhouse chain. }} * {{drink | name=JR Mahon's | alt= | url=https://www.jrmahons.ie/ | email=info@jrmahons.ie | address=1 Burgh Quay D02 F243 | lat= | long= | directions=by O'Connell Bridge | phone=+353 1 670 5777 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-midnight, F-Sa 10:30AM-1AM, Su noon-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Spread over two stories, they produce a very good stout quite different to Guinness, fresher and more complex, plus their own ale and lager. Also has good cafeteria-style lunch. }} * {{drink | name=Porterhouse | alt= | url=https://porterhousebrewco.ie/temple-bar/ | email= | address=16 Parliament St D02 VR94 | lat= | long= | directions=Temple Bar | phone=+353 1 679 8847 | tollfree= | hours=M-W noon-12:30AM, Th-Sa 11AM-1:30AM, Su 10AM-11:30PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Indigenous brews including an oyster stout, plus an extensive Belgian and international beer list. Also does reasonably priced food. Has sister pubs on Grafton St and in Bray and Phibsboro. }}
=== Bars === * {{drink | name=The Foggy Dew | alt= | url=http://www.thefoggydew.ie/ | email= | address=1 Fownes Street, Temple Bar D02 WP21 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 677 9328 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th noon-midnight, F Sa noon-1AM, Su 2PM-midnight | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Popular central Victorian-styled pub. }} * {{drink | name=Bruxelles | alt= | url=https://www.bruxelles.ie/ | email= | address=7 Harry Street D02 KX36 | lat=53.3413 | long=-6.2609 | directions=next to Westbury Hotel | phone=+353 1 677 5362 | tollfree= | hours=Su-W noon-1AM, F Sa noon-2AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Lively pub founded in 1886. Spread over 3 bars the music is loud and the atmosphere is buzzing. Outside is a statue of Phil Lynott, from Irish rock band Thin Lizzy. For metal, rock and indie music go downstairs. }} * {{drink | name=The Duke | alt= | url=http://www.thedukedublin.com/ | email= | address=9 Duke St D02 NR76 | lat=53.3420 | long=-6.2587 | directions= | phone=+353 1 679 9553 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 11AM-11:30PM, F Sa 11AM-12:30AM, Su noon-11PM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Great after-work bar and Fridays it's packed to the door. }} * {{drink | name=O'Donoghues | alt= | url=https://www.odonoghues.ie/ | email= | address=15 Suffolk Street D02 C671 | lat=53.3434 | long=-6.2597 | directions= | phone=+353 85 241 7790 | tollfree= | hours=M-Th 10:30AM-11:30PM, F Sa 10:30AM-12:30AM, Su 11AM-midnight | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=A comfortable bar with live music and TV sport. It's also a hang-out spot for some of the city's most well-known musicians, actors and DJs. }} * {{drink | name=Fibber Magees | alt= | url=http://www.fibbermagees.ie/ | email= | address=80-81 Parnell Street D01 CK74 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 445 5115 | tollfree= | hours=Daily noon-2:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=A heavy metal bar, handy for Rotunda Hospital. }} * {{drink | name=O'Reillys | alt= | url=https://www.oreillys.ie/ | email= | address=Tara St Station, Dublin 2 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 671 6769 | tollfree= | hours=M-W 4-11:30PM, Th-Sa 4PM-12:30AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-07 | content=Victorian Gothic pub beneath the DART station, with Hell Club Saturday late-night music. }}
=== Clubs === * {{drink | name=Button Factory | alt= | url=https://buttonfactory.ie/ | email= | address=Curved St, Temple Bar D02 RD26 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 670 9202 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=One of Dublin's top clubs and live venues. Main venue has 550 capacity, Crowbar is both a theatre bar and a 60-person venue itself. }} * {{drink | name=The Workmans Club | alt= | url=https://theworkmansclub.com/ | email= | address=10 Wellington Quay D02 VX36 | lat= | long= | directions=next to Clarence Hotel | phone=+353 1 670 6692 | tollfree= | hours=Daily 6PM-3AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=From 1888 to 2003 this was the city's Working Mens Club, turning into a live entertainment venue in 2010. It's on two floors, the main live room is 300 capacity and beside it is the venue bar. It's friendly, relaxing and well-run. }} * {{drink | name=The Academy | alt= | url=https://www.theacademydublin.com/ | email= | address=57 Middle Abbey St D01 W573 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 539 2927 | tollfree= | hours= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=This is primarily a live performance venue, and Friday and Saturday are clubbing nights. }} * {{drink | name=Copper Face Jacks | alt= | url=https://copperfacejacks.ie/ | email= | address=29 Harcourt St D02 XV58 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 425 5300 | tollfree= | hours=M-Sa 10AM-2:30AM, Su 10AM-1AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=Legendary club, a post-pub meat-market. Over 20s only, some of the clientele hoping to cop off are going on 120. Rooms available for private events. }} * {{drink | name=The George | alt= | url=http://www.thegeorge.ie/ | email= | address=89 South Great George's St D02 R220 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 478 2983 | tollfree= | hours=M-F 2PM-2:30AM, Sa Su 12:30PM-2AM | price= | lastedit=2024-06-08 | content=The oldest gay bar in Dublin, with late-night drag shows, karaoke, bingo and DJs. }}
== Sleep == {{mapframe|53.345|-6.255|height=300|width=300|name=Dublin accomodations|zoom=10|show=sleep}} [[File:CustomHouseDublin.JPG | thumb | 300px | Cheaper accommodation is north of the Liffey ]] === Camping === : Dublin is not well-served for camping or caravaning. The nearest to the city centre is beyond the M50 to the southwest.
* {{sleep | name=Camac Valley Caravan and Camping Park | alt= | url=https://www.camacvalley.com/ | email=info@camacvalley.com | address=Naas Rd, Clondalkin D022 DR60 | lat=53.3047 | long=-6.4153 | directions=10 km west of city on N7 Naas Rd | phone=+353 1 464 0644 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Tent €12, hook-up €40 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Friendly well-run site on bus route to city. }}
=== Budget === : There are a huge number of youth hostels, bed & breakfasts and hotels. Cheaper accommodation is north of the river, around the Busáras bus station; south of the river is more expensive.
* {{sleep | name=Abbey Court Hostel | alt= | url=https://abbey-court.com | email=info@abbey-court.com | address=29 Bachelors Walk D01 AX90 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 878 0700 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Bunk €35 ppn | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Clean well-run hostel just off O'Connell St. }} * {{sleep | name=Ashling House | alt= | url= | email= | address=168 Drumcondra Rd D09 XH90 | lat=53.3747 | long=-6.2509 | directions= | phone=+353 1 837 5432 | tollfree= | price=Double (room only) €140 | checkin= | checkout= | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Basic accommodation in leafy Drumcondra. Decent rooms but it's not a B&B. }} * {{sleep | name=DCU Summer Rooms | url=https://www.dcurooms.com/ | email= | address= | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 700 5736 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Dublin City University offer spare accommodation to the public all year, with most availability June - September. Their three campuses are Glasnevin, All Hallows in Drumcondra, and St Patrick's in city centre. }} * {{sleep | name=Latroupe Jacobs Inn | alt= | url=https://www.latroupe.com/en/latroupe-jacobs-inn/hostel/ | email= | address=21-28 Talbot St D01 W5P8 | lat=53.35066 | long=-6.25157 | directions=100 m north of bus station | phone=+353 1 855 5660 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Pod €35 ppn | lastedit=2025-07-20 | content=Nice, clean budget hostel with keycard security. Ensuite shower and bathroom as well as an additional washroom at the end of each hall. The bunks are pods so there's extra privacy. }} * {{sleep | name=Lyndon House | alt= | url=https://www.lyndonhouse.net/ | email= | address=26 Gardiner Place D01 Y103 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 878 6555 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €130 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Basic but clean B&B near the James Joyce Museum and the Custom House. }} * {{sleep | name=Travelodge Dublin City Centre | alt= | url=https://www.travelodge.ie/hotels/679/Dublin-City-Centre-hotel | email= | address=44 Townsend St D02 DY01 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 525 9500 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €200 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Reliable chain 3-star, clean and efficient. There are three other Travelodges in the city and one at the airport. }} * {{sleep | name=Premier Inn Gloucester Street South | alt= | url=https://www.premierinn.com/gb/en/hotels/republic-of-ireland/dublin/dublin/dublin-city-centre-gloucester-street-south.html | email= | address=29 Gloucester St South D02 WC98 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 636 9060 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €150 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Value-for-money chain hotel. There are three other Premier Inns in the city and one at the airport. }} * {{sleep | name=Trinity College | alt= | url=https://www.visittrinity.ie/stay/ | email= | address=TCD Campus | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 896 1177 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price= | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=TCD accommodation is available May-Sep in single, double or apartment rooms, some en suite. There is limited access to the campus after midnight, which can result in a long walk from the main gate to some of the residences. }} * {{sleep | name=Generator Dublin | alt= | url=https://staygenerator.com/hostels/dublin | email=dublin@generatorhostels.com | address=Smithfield Square D07 F2VF | lat= | long= | directions=by distillery | phone=+353 1 901 0222 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=Bunk €40 ppn | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Smart hostel. Open social spaces but also a bar and a café to its guests. There are male and female shared rooms that come with all facilities, plus private rooms. Group bookings and private hires available. }}
=== Mid-range === [[File:Merrion Square - Oscar Wilde by Danny Osborne (1997) (5495420830).jpg | thumb | 300px | Oscar Wild looking wasted in Merrion Square ]] * {{sleep | name=Albany House | alt= | url=https://www.albanyhousedublin.com | email= | address=84 Harcourt St D02 Y045 | lat= | long= | directions=100 m south of St Stephens Green | phone=+353 1 475 1092 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €180 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Good central 3-star, some noise in street-facing rooms. Shower and taps take 5 min to run hot, keep running and have faith. }} * {{sleep | name=Ariel House | alt= | url=https://www.ariel-house.net/ | email= | address=50-54 Lansdowne Rd, Ballsbridge D04 DD27 | lat= | long= | directions=by Aviva Stadium | phone=+353 1 668 5512 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €180 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Comfy welcoming B&B near Aviva stadium. === Airport === [[File:What to wear (18007218671).jpg | thumb | 300px | When in doubt, quote Joyce ]] : Dublin Airport is north of the city near the town of [[Swords]]. Hotels listed here are so close to the airport that you'd travel that way to reach them, even if you weren't flying, and many inter-city buses run via the airport. Those closer to Swords town centre are listed on that page along with other amenities: they're much cheaper than their airport or city centre equivalents.
* {{sleep | name=Maldron Hotel | alt= | url=https://www.maldronhoteldublinairport.com | email= | address=Corballis Way, Dublin Airport K67 T6P6 | lat= | long= | directions= | phone=+353 1 808 0500 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €200 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Mid-price hotel opposite Radisson Blu. }} * {{sleep | name=Radisson Blu Hotel Dublin Airport | alt= | url=https://www.radissonhotels.com/en-us/hotels/radisson-blu-dublin-airport | email= | address=Corballis Way, Dublin Airport K67 H5H9 | lat= | long= | directions=200 m east of T2 | phone=+353 1 844 6000 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €240 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Comfy accommodation within the airport complex just minutes from the passenger terminals. }} * {{sleep | name=Clayton Hotel Dublin Airport | alt= | url=https://www.claytonhoteldublinairport.com/ | email=info.dublinairport@claytonhotels.com | address=Stockhole Lane, Swords K67 X3H5 | lat=53.4117 | long=-6.2182 | directions=At jcn M50 / M1 exit for R139 Malahide, don't take airport off-ramp | phone=+353 1 871 1000 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €200 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Convenient mid-range hotel with free shuttle to the airport. }} * '''Holiday Inn Express''' next to the Clayton in 2024 is refugee accommodation. * {{sleep | name=Carlton Hotel | alt= | url=https://www.carltondublinairport.com/| email= | address=Old Airport Rd, Cloghran K67 P5C7 | lat= | long= | directions=1 km south of airport | phone=+353 1 866 7500 | tollfree= | checkin= | checkout= | price=B&B double €180 | lastedit=2024-06-05 | content=Mid-price hotel with free shuttle to the airport. Bar-food menu and a restaurant on the top floor. }}
== Stay safe == : Dublin is a safe city by day, but keep your wits about you, especially as night draws on. The emergency number for all services is 999 or 112.
* '''Traffic:''' the city is congested, avoid bringing a car yourself, and never leave valuables on display in a car. : Motorists often ignore pedestrian crossing lights. Maybe they're preoccupied with searching for a parking spot. * '''Street life:''' Buskers, drunks and down-and-outs are all part of the picaresque Dublin scene, and ''An Béal Bocht'' - "the poor mouth" - has a noble literary tradition. A polite "sorry" should see them off. : Gardaí (police officers) are happy to assist or give directions. If you fear for your safety and can't find or phone a Garda, head to the nearest shop, bar etc where you will be safe and can call. Pub doormen and bouncers will likewise call the police for you, they want to forestall trouble on their street. * '''LGBTQ+ travellers:''' Dublin is usually not only safe but welcoming, however 2022 saw a 29% increase nationwide in hate crimes against LGBTQ+ people. Each Garda station has a Diversity Officer on duty, and the police are vigilant against all forms of hate crime. Support can also be obtained from [https://www.outhouse.ie/ Outhouse LGBTQ+ Centre] on Capel Street, tel +353 1 873 4999. * '''Rough areas:''' Boardwalk, Lower Abbey Street and around Connolly station are tacky rather than unsafe by day, worth a swerve at night. : You have simply no reason to be in down-at-heel outlying districts such as Ballymun.
== Cope == [[File:Molly WHO (49974151198).jpg | thumb | 300px | Molly Malone in 2020, avoiding dying of a fever ]] === Medical === You should only go to the hospital if you're too ill to get there, so to speak. For immediate treatment of minor ailments try one of the '''Walk-in Medical Centres.''' The most central are [https://damestreetmedical.ie/ Dame Street Medical] at 16 Dame St in Temple Bar, [https://www.abbeymedicalcentre.com/ Abbey Street Centre] at 71 Middle Abbey St, and [https://www.24doc.ie/ 24Doc] at North Wall behind the Customs House. They're private so an EHIC / GHIC card won't help. Expect to pay €60 for a consultation, plus the cost of any prescription or other treatment.

== Connect ==
As of July 2024, all of the city and its approach roads have 5G from all Irish carriers.
Dublin has over 20 [https://www.dublincity.ie/residential/libraries/find-library public libraries]. These have free wifi and internet access, but you need to register as a user to access. This may be possible for non-residents with extended stays.
== Go next == [[File:Howth Cliff Walk.jpg|alt=Howth's Cliff Walk, Ireland | thumb | 300px | Cliff walk, Howth ]] Almost all of Ireland is within 2-3 hours travel from Dublin, and the transport routes converge on the city. People even make day-trips to the [[Aran Islands]] out west or [[Giant's Causeway]] in the north, a mad way to experience them. Those listed here are all within an hour of the city. * '''[[County Dublin]]:''' : - [[Dun Laoghaire]] is the city's former harbour, and [[Dalkey]] is a prosperous seaside suburb with Riveria architecture. : - [[Howth]] is a scenic peninsula. Boat trips sail to Ireland's Eye, teeming with bird life. : - [[Malahide]] has a fine castle. A walk along the coast brings you to Portmarnock beach. * '''[[County Meath]]:''' : - [[Brú Na Bóinne Archaeological Park]] has the megalithic tombs of Newgrange, Knowth and Dowth. * '''[[County Wicklow]]:''' : - [[Enniskerry]] has the gardens of Powerscourt and the highest waterfall in Ireland. : - [[Glendalough]] is a remarkable monastic village in a scenic mountain valley.
